paragraphs.body: What it means to be a boy is when you can cry to you mom or someone else about your problems. It Means that you know that you always have someone that is there for you. It is the little things like playing catch with your dad or just riding your bike with your group of friends in the neighborhood. It is the things that you do not even notice until you are older. It is the way we live without caring about a thing. The way we just eat whatever we want and do not have a care about calories. It the way we think not having to worry about money or how we are goanna get dinner today. Overall life is just so simple that we take it for granted when we get older, and life becomes harder.

annotations.context_left: g catch with your dad or just riding your bike with your group of friends in the neighborhood. It is

paragraphs.body: It was my 8th grade year going into my first year and I got the news that my papa was in the hospital with covid and before he got covid he had stage 3 liver cancer so that was already kicking him in the ass. I was sad and depressed. I did not want to do anything because he was my dad and every little thing that I did somehow always remind me of him. Then I would get all sad again and just want to sit in my room with my teddy bear even though I was in 8th grade when I was still a child. My teddy bear was the only thing ever there for me when I was going through this. I did not want to talk to anyone about my problems or just tell someone how I feel because that is what my grandpa did, and I wanted to live in his shoes and become the great and amazing man he became. I really could deal with my emotions and the only thing that would help me in a sense was just to hug my teddy bear. I know, trust me, that sounds childish for an 8th grader to do that is about to become a first-year student in high school. But it was the only thing that I had. I did not have anyone. I really did not have any friends; I was never really invited too much. So, I just sat in my room and played on my PS4. The only things I did was train for wrestling and football and honestly without sports. I have no idea how I would have gotten through this without my teddy and sports because it keeps my mind distracted. My teddy bear is soft and cuddly, it is warm with brown fur that feels gentle to the touch. It has eyes that are round, and it gives a little shin. But the shin is starting to go away as the teddy bear gets old. It gives the presence of a friend, a caring friend. It looks cute and comforting, like you can hug it for days. Its arms and legs are floppy, which is perfect for hugging. The whole body is the perfect shape and right size to hold close. Over time the fur becomes a little worn but that only makes it feel more special because it has memories in it. When you hug it there is a soft sweet scent that reminds you of home and safety. When it is just resting on a shelf or resting on your bed it is always there to comfort you, making it a true friend. It also has a small, stitched nose and a wide friendly smile that makes you to be happy. Even though it lost a couple threads, and the fur is a little worn it is still always there when you are alone and just need a hug. The bear is a true friend offering love and support whenever you need it or when you are feeling down, or you just need a little warmth. Every time I go home and see the teddy bear; I had for years it reminds me of the memories from my childhood and now that when I am alone and trying to deal with my emotions it helps me in a way to think about those old memories and saying it will be okay it will all work out.

But it was the only thing that I had. I did not have anyone.

paragraphs.body: The problem nowadays is in our new culture we have this perception of what a man is. My grandfather is an exact representation of that. He never cried or talked to anyone about his problems. It is like that he had the Great Wall of China around his feelings, and he did not let anyone in. This is why when he died, I wanted to become the man he wanted to be, and I did that, but it costs me a lot. It made it so that when I got older and grew up, I would never tell anyone or anything about my problems and I would just bottle them up and act like they do not matter. The one thing that I realized is that our culture is to blame for this because of traditional ideas about masculinity like fear of being judged, the belief that showing emotions is a sign of weakness, and how media shows men as stoic and strong. All these things add up and it gets to a breaking point that men eventually just kill them shelf because they feel like they do not have anyone, and they feel like they cannot deal with their emotions, so they take the easy way out. Thats why the suicide rate among men in the U.S. is about four times that of women! But that is not what being a man means. Being a real man means talking about your feelings, not bottling them up or acting like you do not care. It means leaving yourself open to people, not stuffing your emotions down and pretending they are invalid. Being a real man means allowing yourself to be your true self, not hiding behind a fake mask and acting like you have no soul. Being a real man means being honest, vulnerable, and true to who you are without fear or shame. It means standing up for yourself while also listening to others. It means showing kindness, compassion, and strength without feeling like you are some sorry sacks of shit. Being a real man means learning to accept help when you need it and allowing others to support you without feeling like you must do everything on your own and the entire world has its back turned on you. It means learning from mistakes instead of pretending they never happened and growing through challenges rather than running from them. Being a real man means breaking the cycle of toxic masculinity and choosing to define your own strength, NOT ANYONE ELSES BUT YOUR OWN. It means showing your emotions as they come, being open about your fears, joys, and dreams, and proving that having feelings does not make you any less of a man. If anything IT MAKES YOU A REAL MAN.
